steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to emphasize the aesthetic qualities of the work. Ortiz's key phrase is 'charm and a warmth and a beauty' — any correct answer must bring that language in.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — The handmade, "imperfect" look of stop motion animation, says Ortiz, lends this "undeniably human" technique a "charm and a warmth and a beauty.": uses Ortiz's exact aesthetic descriptors. ✓
  • Choice B — As Ortiz says, "you effectively get one opportunity to get it right" when meticulously capturing the series of images that, when played back, give it the illusion of motion.: focuses on technical difficulty of the craft, not aesthetic qualities. ✗
  • Choice C — With its puppets and clay figures, stop motion animation, Ortiz says, "dates back to the dawn of cinema.": focuses on historical origin of the tradition, not aesthetics. ✗
  • Choice D — Stop motion animation is imperfect, making every shot "a high-wire act," Ortiz says.: focuses on the production process, not aesthetic qualities. ✗
That is the correct answer.
